etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "tap; strike") -> warning or purification action -> evolved to include drops suggesting water for purification -> final form
etymology.interpretation: 攸(攸) (yōu, "distant") originally represented either a warning action with authority or a purification involving water, evolving over time from practical gestures to more abstract meanings.
